title: Observability contract alignment, threshold normalization, and shipment resilience policy wiring
description: Resolved this session's remaining acceptance gaps by aligning frontend/backend alert event contracts, normalizing threshold operators across admin and observability APIs, enforcing idempotency on alert acknowledgement, wiring shipment retry/backoff policy from config into scheduler and worker behavior, and synchronizing documentation and tests to the implemented behavior.

errors.error: Real-time alert contract mismatch between emitted event/payload and frontend listener expectations
errors.cause: Backend emitted alert:threshold-breach with metricName while frontend primarily consumed threshold:breach with metric field assumptions.
errors.fix: Standardized frontend handling for alert:threshold-breach and preserved backward compatibility with threshold:breach; backend payload now includes metric and canonical operator context.

errors.error: Threshold operator drift across admin settings, threshold APIs, and UI
errors.cause: Mixed operator representations (gt/lt/gte/lte vs symbolic operators) created inconsistent validation and persistence behavior.
errors.fix: Introduced shared operator normalization utility with alias support and applied canonical operator handling across validation, persistence, and UI defaults/options.

errors.error: Alert acknowledgement mutation was missing idempotency protection
errors.cause: PATCH /api/alerts/:id/acknowledge route did not include idempotency middleware despite being a mutating endpoint.
errors.fix: Applied idempotency middleware to alert acknowledgement route and added explicit API test coverage for missing-key enforcement and route inclusion in idempotency matrix.

errors.error: Shipment sync retry/backoff policy existed in config but was not wired consistently into queue scheduling and execution
errors.cause: Repeat interval and retry behavior were partly hardcoded and not uniformly sourced from shipmentSync config.
errors.fix: Added shared shipment sync policy helpers, wired configurable repeat interval, mapped retries to BullMQ attempts, introduced custom backoff strategy, and reused policy in manual carrier-sync trigger.

errors.error: Circuit-breaker operational policy and operator-facing alert surfacing drift
errors.cause: Breaker timing/window defaults and alerting did not reflect intended resilience policy visibility.
errors.fix: Exposed configurable breaker timing/window parameters and emitted system:alert notifications when circuits open and recover.

errors.error: Seeded threshold metric names and documentation references were stale relative to current implementation
errors.cause: Seed data and docs still referenced outdated metric/operator/event/schedule assumptions.
errors.fix: Updated seeded thresholds to collected metric names with canonical operators and synchronized API spec, acceptance checklist, and test-plan wording to implemented contracts.

rootCause: Cross-module contract drift and partially wired resilience policies: event payloads, operator representations, and retry/breaker behavior diverged between implementation paths, tests, and documentation.
impact: Before this fix set, threshold alerts risked inconsistent client behavior, admin/operator threshold updates could be interpreted differently across endpoints, alert acknowledgement lacked replay protection, and shipment resilience behavior was less deterministic against configured retry policy.

validation.method: Dockerized targeted validation from PowerShell using compose test stack with setup/teardown
validation.result: All targeted suites passed for the changed areas
